items_fr: 6.  That the Secretary-General is  requested to submit a  report to the next regular session of the General Assembly on the action taken under this resolution.

text_en: Take note of the report of the Secretary› General on the staff rules and amendments thereto which he had promoted to implement the Provisional Staff Regulatory (d()(utn<"nt A/435);

text_fr: Requests the Secretary-General to present, four months prior to the third regular session  of the General Assembly, a  codification of the staff rules for the information of the Assembly;
text_en: Requests the Secretary-General to present, four months prior to the third regular session of the General Assembly, a codification of the staff rules for the information of the Assembly;

text_fr: Resolves that the Provisional Staff Regulations relating  to  children's allowances  and  education grants  (regulations  30,  31,  32,  33  and  34)  be cancelled and superseded, with effect from 1 Jan› uary 1948, by the amended regulations contained in  Annex A, and
text_en: Resolve that the Provisional Staff Regulations relating to children's allowances and education grants (regulations 30, 31, 32, 33 and 34) be cancelled and superseded, with effect from 1 January 1948, by the amended regulations contained in Annex A, and

text_fr: Resolves that the Provisional Staff Regulations relating to appointment, probation and promotion be amended by the addition of regulation  12  A and that regulation 21  be revised, as contained in Annex B.
text_en: Resolve that the Provisional Staff Regulations relating to supplement, probation and promotion be amended by the addition of regulation 12 A and that regulation 21 be revised, as contained in Annex B.

text_fr: The allowance  shall  continue  to  be  payable  in respect  of  his  children  to  a  full-·ime  member  of the  staff  who  becomes entitled  under  the  United Nations  Joint  Staff  Pension  Fund  Regulations  to a  retirement or a  disability benefit and to a  widow if in receipt of a  widow benefit.
text_en: The allowance will continue to be payable in respect of his children to a full-·ime member of the staff who come to be entitled under the United Nations Joint Staff Pension Fund Regulations to a withdrawal or a disability benefit and to a bidow if in receipt of a bidow benefit.

text_fr: Regulation 32
text_en: Regulation 32

text_fr: Upon the death of a  wember of  t'1e staff who receives  a  children's allowance  under  these  regu› lations,  and  following  the  death  of  the  other parent, there shall be paid to the legal guardian of each child  an  allowance  of  $US400,  or  such other appropriate amount as  may be fixed  by the United  Nations  Staff  Pension  Committee,  having regard to  the further proviso in regulation 30.
text_en: Upon the death of a member of t'1e staff who receive a child's allowance under these regulations, and following the death of the other parent, there will be paid to the legal guardian of each child an allowance of $US400, or such other appropriate amount as may be fixed by the United Nations Staff Pension Committee, having regard to the further provide in regulation 30.

text_fr: Regulation 33
text_en: Regulation 33

text_fr: Each  full-time  member  of  the  staff,  with  the exception of those specifically excluded by a  reso› lution of the General Assembly, entitled to receive a  children's allowance under regulation 30,  who is employed  by  the  United  Nations  in  a  country other  than  his  own  country  as  specified  in  his letter of appointment, shall be entitled to the fol› lowing  education  grant:
text_en: Each full-time member of the staff, with the exception of those specifically excluded by a reso› loan of the General Assembly, entitled to receive a child's allowance under regulation 30, who is employed by the United Nations in a country other than his own country as specified in his letter of supplement, shall be entitled to the fol› lowing education grant:

items_fr: ( a) The  sum  of  $US200  per  annum  for each child, in  respect of whom a  children's allow› ance is  payable, in full-time attendance at a  school or a  university in his home country; provided that, where  a  child  attt'nded such an  educational  insti› tution  for a  period of less  than  two-thirds  of any one scholastic year, the allowance shall be reduced to  such  proportion  of  $US200  as  the  period so  attended bear~  to a  full  scholastic  year;
( b) Once in each  scholastic  year the  travelling expenses  of  the  outward  and  return  journey  of such a  child by a  route approved by the Sc-cretary› General;

( c) Should  staff  members  elect  to  send  their children  to  special  national  schools  in  the  area wherc  thr-y  are  scTving,  including  intC'rnational schools  organized  for  children  of  United  Nations staff members, rather than to schools in  their home countries,  the·  United  Nations  will  pay  for  each child  otherwise  eligible  for  the  education  grant, an  allowance  equal  to  the difference  betwecll  the cost  of  education  at  the  special  school  which  he attends  and  thc  cost  at a comparable  school  at› tcnded by children of persons normally resident in the  area,  provided  that  the  allowance  shall  not exceed  $US200  per  year. This  allowance  shall be  payable  only  when  there  is a valid  reason  for the child not to attend school in the home country; for  instance,  in  thC'  case  of  children  under  eleven years  of  age  or  when  the  health  of  the  child  is such  that  return  to  the  home  country  is  not feasible.
items_en: (a) The sum of $US200 for annum for each child, in respect of who a child's allowance›ance is payable, in full-time expectation at a school or a university in his home country; provided that, where a child att'nded such an educational insti› tution for a period of less than two-thirds of any one school year, the allowance shall be reduced to such proportion of $US200 as the period so attendedear~ to a full school year;
(b) Once in each scholastic year the travelling expenses of the outward and return journey of such a child by a road approved by the Sc-cretary› General;

(c) Should staff members elect to send their children to special national schools in the area where when three are scTving, including intC'rnational schools organized for children of United Nations staff members, other than to schools in their home countries, the· United Nations will pay for each child otherwise eligible for the education grant, an allowance equal to the difference between the costs of education at the special school which he attends and thc cost at a comparable school at› tcnded by children of persons normally resident in the area, provided that the allowance shall not exceed $US200 per year. This allowance shall be payable only when there is a valid reason for the child not residing in the area, provided that the allowance not exceed $US200 per year. This allowance shall be payable only when there is a valid reason for the child not to wait for school in the child.

text_fr: If both parents arc members of the  staff of  the United  Nations,  only  onc  grant  will  be  paid  in respect of each  of their children.
text_en: If both parents arc members of the staff of the United Nations, only onc grant will be paid in respect of each of their children.

text_fr: The  Secretary-General  may  terminate  the  ap› pointment of a  member of the staff in accordance with  the  terms  of his  appointment if made under the  provisions  of regulation  12A,  or if  the  neces› sities  of  the  service  require  the  abolition  of  the post  or a  reduction  of the staff,  or if  the  services of  the  individual  concerned  prove  unsatisfactory.
text_en: The Secretary-General may terminate the ap› pointment of a member of the staff in accordance with the terms of his supplement if made under the provisions of regulation 12A, or if the needs› cities of the service requires the abolition of the post or a reduction of the staff, or if the services of the individual concerned prove unsatisfactory.

text_fr: L'engagement  de  tout  membre  du  personnel pour  une  periode  de  stage  ou  sur  la  base  d'un contrat a court terme, ee  qui s'applique a tous  Jes contrats  temporaires,  peut etre soumis  awe  condi› tions  que  le  Secretaire  general estime  opportunes.
text_en: The appointment of any staff member for a period of probation or on the basis of a short-term contract, which applies to all temporary contracts, may be subject to conditions which the Secretary-General considers appropriate.
